etymology.description: Depicts four parallel lines -> symbolizing the number four -> used before 口 (kǒu) components were added in 四 (四)
etymology.interpretation: The character 亖 (sǐ, "four") originated as an ideogram representing four parallel lines to signify the number four. It became obsolete after the Qin dynasty's writing standardization, with 四 (sì, "four") replacing it to avoid confusion with 二 (èr, "two") and 三 (sān, "three") in vertical writing formats.
